data.title: The Week That Was, The Week Ahead: Macro and Markets, Feb. 15
data.description:
U.S. stocks ended the week lower as investors moved away from big tech and focused more on safer areas of the market. The S&P 500 (SPX) fell 1.28%, the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) dropped 1.27%, and the Dow (DJIA) slid 1.15%. At the same time, the 10-year Treasury yield eased to about 4.09%, while gold (CM:XAUUSD) jumped above $5,000 and oil (CM:CL) held near $63 a barrel.

data.title: Tesla Stock: Energy Boom vs. EV Bust – Can FSD Drive 2026 Recovery?
data.description:
Shares of electric vehicle (EV) giant Tesla (TSLA) traded around $411-$420 in early 2026, down 7% year-to-date amid EV sales declines. Yet, Tesla's most bullish analyst eyes potential 40%+ upside from energy storage growth and Full Self-Driving (FSD) monetization.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

EV Challenges Weigh Heavy

Tesla's Q4 FY25 deliveries dropped 15% year-over-year to 418,000 units, marking a second annual decline. Fierce competition from Chinese rival BYD (BYDDF) and softening demand in Europe, where registrations fell over 40%, have led to the declines. Leadership shake-ups, including a new global sales head, highlight internal efforts to counter eroding market share and margin-squeezing price wars.

Energy Business Powers Ahead

Energy storage deployments hit records in 2025, positioning Megapack and Powerwall for $12 to $15 billion in revenue by 2027, diversifying beyond vehicles hit by macroeconomic headwinds. This high-margin segment could offset auto weakness, with 2025 revenue at $95 billion despite a 2.9% dip. Investors see it as a stabilizing force amid Tesla's pivot to software-led growth.

FSD and Robotics to the Rescue

Tesla's FSD subscriptions could generate $50-$100 monthly per vehicle, potentially lifting margins to 13%-20% by 2028 if regulatory approvals accelerate. Tesla's $20 billion 2026 capex guidance targets autonomous driving and Optimus robots. CEO Elon Musk plans to end production of premium Model S and Model X at its California factories to reallocate capacity to Optimus.

Analysts' Views on Tesla Stock

Tigress Financial analyst Ivan Feinseth initiated coverage with a Buy rating and $550 price target, implying 31.9% upside potential. He sees Tesla shifting its focus from a pure-play EV maker to a broad AI platform featuring self-driving tech. He noted that growth will come from more FSD subscriptions, robotaxis, and Optimus robots, adding to its strong EV and energy businesses.

Morgan Stanley analyst Andrew Percoco maintained his Hold rating and $415 price target, suggesting that shares are fully valued at current levels. He sees Tesla's solar manufacturing push as a smart long-term bet on geopolitics and data center demand. At full capacity, Tesla Solar could add $20 billion to $50 billion in value ($6 to $14 per share) to its $140 billion energy business ($40 per share). Although not huge on a standalone basis, vertical integration could boost growth.

Is Tesla Stock a Good Buy Right Now?

Analysts remain cautious about Tesla's long-term prospects. On TipRanks, TSLA stock has a Hold consensus rating based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sell ratings. The average Tesla price target of $396.80 implies 4.9% downside potential from current levels. Over the past year, TSLA shares have gained 17%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Top Analysts Are Bullish on Nvidia Stock (NVDA) Ahead of Q4 Earnings
data.description:
Semiconductor giant Nvidia (NVDA) is scheduled to announce its results for the fourth quarter of Fiscal 2026 on February 25. NVDA stock has risen 35% over the past year but is down nearly 2% so far in 2026 amid concerns about growing competition and fears about an AI (artificial intelligence) bubble. Nevertheless, top Wall Street analysts remain bullish on Nvidia due to its solid execution, robust demand for its AI GPUs (graphics processing units), and continued innovation.

Meanwhile, Wall Street expects Nvidia to report earnings per share (EPS) of $1.52, reflecting 71% year-over-year growth. Revenue is projected to rise 67% to $65.58 billion.

data.title: Fear Robinhood's Post-Earnings Slump? Analysts Say 'Frustrating' But Stay Bullish
data.description:
Robinhood Markets (HOOD), the popular retail trading platform, saw its stock plunge nearly 17% over the past two days after mixed Q4 results on February 10. Earnings per share (EPS) of $0.66 beat analysts' consensus estimate of $0.63 per share, but sales of $1.28 billion missed the $1.35 billion consensus forecast. The shortfall mainly came from a 38% drop in crypto transaction revenue to $221 million, driven by lower trading volumes.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

HOOD shares are down 37% year-to-date. The key question: Will this downward spiral continue, or are downside risks easing? Analysts offer a mixed but mostly optimistic view.

Analysts Trim Targets But Stay Bullish

Truist Financial analyst David Smith cut his price target from $130 to $120, still implying 68.7% upside potential, but kept his Buy rating. He called the Q4 report "frustrating," stating that outside of prediction markets, Robinhood had few positives. Key numbers like user growth and net deposits were weak in December alongside crypto weakness, while January was a bit better. He remains optimistic about Robinhood's long-term potential, despite short-term volatility.

Similarly, Needham analyst John Todaro slashed his price target to $100 (from $135), suggesting 40.6% upside, while keeping his Buy view. He noted that Q4 results were strong and met his estimates. Prediction markets particularly stood out, with January volumes hitting a record $3.5 billion in contracts. However, crypto volumes and take rates weakened in fiscal 26, along with options and equities. He expects crypto weakness to linger for two quarters before recovering.

Additionally, Piper Sandler analyst Patrick Moley kept his Buy rating but trimmed his price target from $155 to $135, implying 89.8% upside. Moley sees short-term headwinds but keeps his long-term view intact. "If you can stomach the volatility, HOOD is the best way to play secular growth in retail trading and the closest FinTech platform we've ever seen to achieving 'super app' status," Moley argued.

Key Takeaway

Analysts' concerns mostly center around short-term crypto and trading volume volatility, which make up a big chunk of Robinhood's revenue (crypto alone was about 17% of Q4 total). Yet, all three analysts see strong long-term potential in Robinhood's low-cost brokerage, expanding products like prediction markets, and retail trading growth.

Is HOOD a Long-Term Investment?

On TipRanks, HOOD stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 14 Buys and two Hold ratings. The average Robinhood Markets price target of $135.46 implies 90.5% upside potential from current levels.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: With Micron Stock (MU) Up 285%, Let's Look at Who Owns It
data.description:
Micron Technology (MU) stock rose 285% in 2025 and is up another 44% year to date, driven by strong data-center demand for High Bandwidth Memory (HBM) and server DRAM tied to the artificial intelligence (AI) boom. In its latest fiscal first-quarter (November quarter) report, revenue was $13.64 billion, up 57% year over year, and adjusted EPS was $4.78, above estimates of $3.96. As demand for chips improves, analysts have been updating their outlooks, making it worth looking at who owns Micron shares.

data.title: Are Quantum Stocks Worth Buying Now? Analysts Say 'Yes' for These 3 Names
data.description:
Quantum computing stocks are back on investors' radar in 2026 after last year's rally and the pullback that followed. Most companies are still in an early stage, with limited revenue and a focus on improving their systems and reducing errors. Even so, government funding and partnerships with large tech companies continue to support interest in the sector, though the stocks remain high-risk.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

To find such stocks, take a look at TipRanks' Quantum Computing Stocks page. It allows you to compare stocks based on analyst consensus, price targets, and key technical indicators, among others. Today, we have picked stocks that carry an Outperform Smart Score (i.e., 8, 9, or 10) on TipRanks, which indicates that these stocks have the potential to beat the market.

Here are today's top quantum computing stock picks. Click on any ticker to thoroughly research the stock before you decide whether to add it to your portfolio.

1. Microsoft (MSFT) – Microsoft is working on quantum computing through its Azure Quantum platform and research teams. The company is trying to build reliable quantum systems using its "topological qubit" design, which aims to cut errors and make the technology usable. For now, Microsoft lets developers and companies test quantum programs and run simulations through Azure while full-scale machines are still in development.

For investors, Microsoft offers indirect exposure to quantum computing, backed by its strong cloud, software, and AI businesses rather than near-term quantum sales.

MSFT stock has an analyst consensus of Strong Buy, 47.86% upside potential, and a Smart Score of Eight.

2. Alphabet (GOOGL) – Alphabet, the parent of Google, is developing quantum computing through its Google Quantum AI team. Rather than aiming for near-term revenue, the company is focused on research, especially improving qubit stability and reducing errors so systems can perform reliable calculations.

Google has already shared results from tests using its superconducting chips and continues to work with universities and research partners. For investors, Alphabet gives exposure to quantum computing, but the stock is still supported mainly by its search, cloud, and AI businesses rather than any quantum income today.

GOOGL stock has an analyst consensus of Strong Buy, 23.39% upside potential, and a Smart Score of "Perfect 10."

3. D-Wave Quantum (QBTS) – QBTS takes a different path from most quantum firms. It uses quantum annealing, which targets optimization problems rather than general computing. Its systems are being used for route planning, job scheduling, and supply-chain work, where faster calculations can save time and cost.

Recently, the company has been expanding use of its Advantage systems and its hybrid quantum-classical software, allowing businesses to run real tasks instead of simple tests. Because of this, D-Wave already has paying customers and active projects, while many other quantum companies are still mostly in the research stage. Its focus on near-term uses gives it a more practical position than many early-stage peers.

QBTS stock has an analyst consensus of Strong Buy, 109.71% upside potential, and a Smart Score of Nine.
data.author: Shalu Saraf
